rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL BYKHOVSKY et al VS. WILLIAM PORTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses From X-Deft Walsh To (1) Request For Production Of Documents And (2)Special Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 18, 2013, Line 1, CROSS COMPLAINANT WILLIAM PORTER's Motion To Compel Further Responses From X-Deft Walsh To (1) Request For Production Of Documents And (2)Special Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge David May, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: With respect to Doc Req. 24-72, denied. Too broad, i.e. not reasonably particularized within the meaning of C.C.P. §2031.030 and, due to private nature of information, must be directly relevant to a material issue raised in the pleadings and must be narrowly and specifically drawn. Britt v. Superior Court (1978) 20 Cal.3d 844, 855;Life Technologies Corp. v. Superior Court (Timothy H. Joyce) (2011) 197 Cal.App.4th 640, 653. To the extent that Req. 72 requests documents recorded in Nevada, those documents are a matter of public record and are equally available to propounding party. With respect to the special interrogatories: Nos. 3, 4, 6, 7, 8, 9, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20, 24, 28 denied; Nos. 10, 12, 29, 30, 31, 35, 40, 41, 44, 46 granted in part, responding party shall identifiy all responsive documents not already identified pursuant to CCP §2030.230 if any; No. 39 granted in part, the term YOU is limited to cross-defendant William Walsh. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidedwardmay@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSITION: $1,916.00 U.S. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Terminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 18, 2013, Line 2, PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA's Motion For Terminating Sanctions Pro Tem Judge David May, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied as moot. Judgment in favor of Petitioner/Moving Party/People has already been entered on October 1, 2012. Court has no jurisdiction to issue requested order. (C.C.P. §1049; Mason & Associates, Inc. v. Guarantee Sav. & Loan Ass'nof Livermore Valley (1969) 269 Cal.App.2d 132, 133-4.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidedwardmay@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: ERNEST LINCOLN BONNER JR. M.D VS. THE MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On 1st Amended Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 18, 2013, Line 4: PETITIONER ERNEST BONNER JR.'S 1st Amended Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus Continued to January 24, 2014 to give Petitioner the opportunity to comply with CRC 3.1110(c), (e) and (f), and Local Rule 2.6B and 2.6C. Plaintiff must deliver courtesy copies of: (1) the Second Amended Petition; (2) his moving papers and declarations filed on October 17, 2013; and (3) the Pretrial Brief filed on December 11 2013 to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than January 3, 2014 at 4:00 pm or the petition will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). No later than January 10, 2014 at 4 pm, Plaintiff shall lodge an administrative record that is in compliance with CRC 3.1110(c), (e) and (f). "Documents bound together must be consecutively paginated." (CRC 3.1110(c).) "All pages of each document and exhibit must be attached together at the top by a method that permits pages to be easily turned and the entire content of each page to be read." (CRC 3.1110(e).) "Each exhibit must be separated by a hard 8 ½ x 11 sheet with hard paper or plastic tabs extending below the bottom of the page, bearing the exhibit designation." (CRC 3.1110(f).) "An index to exhibits must be provided." (Ibid.) Plaintiff may pick up from Department 302 the administrative record lodged on December 11, 2013 and re-lodge it in compliance with CRC 3.1110(c), (e) and (f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. SEAVIEW INSURANCE CO.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Relief From Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 18, 2013, Line 7: REAL PARTY IN INTEREST SEAVIEW INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion For Relief From Summary Judgment Denied. Seaview Insurance Company did not file a timely motion within the appearance period under Penal Code § 1305, and the Court entered summary judgment in accordance with Penal Code § 1306. Consequently, the Court lacks jurisdiction to set aside the summary judgment based on Seaview's mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect pursuant to CCP § 473. (Compare People v. North River Insurance Company (2011) 200 Cal.App.4th 712 with People v. Stuyvesant Ins. Co. (1963) 216 Cal.App.2d 380.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: MARK PRINGLE COMPANY LLC, VS. NORRIED FAMILY PROPERTIES LLP,
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 18, 2013, Line 8: PETITIONER MARK PRINGLE COMPANY LLC'S Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award The Court grants Mark Pringle Company, LLC's Petition to Confirm Arbitration Award and denies Norried Family Properties, L.P. ("Norried") Petition to Vacate Arbitration Award. Norried fails to establish a basis to vacate the award. The parties submitted the issue regarding whether Norried Family Properties, L.P. performed paragraph 3.6.1 of the parties' agreement and whether such performance would have been illegal. The arbitrator ruled on the issue and the decision is final. See Moncharsh v. Heily & Blase (1992) 3 Cal.4th 1, 11 and 30. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: YVONNE JANSKY VS. LABORATORY CORPORATION OF AMERICA
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 18, 2013, Line 9: DEFENDANTS LABORATORY CORPORATION OF AMERICA'S and LABORATORY CORPORATION OF AMERICA HOLDINGS' DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT The Court sustains the demurrer to causes of action 2 and 3 without leave to amend. Plaintiff does not sufficiently allege actionable misrepresentations and the claims lack the requisite specificity. The Court sustains the demurrer to cause of action 10 without leave to amend. See CCP 1061. The Court sustains the demurrer to cause of action 11 without leave to amend. Plaintiff does not allege outrageous conduct. The Court overrules the demurrers to the remaining causes of action. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges injury/damage, including dimunition of value and benefits under the insurance contract. Plaintiff alleges that defendant concealed information, disrupted her relationship with her insurer, engaged in unfair competition, and engaged in unfair debt collection practices. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Calendar Matter: Claim Of Expemption; Notice Of Non-Oppos
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 18, 2013, Line 15: PLAINTIFF CACH, LLC'S Claim Of Expemption; Notice Of Non-Opposition Judgment Debtor Palmer's claim of exemption is denied. Judgment Debtor has not carried her burden of showing all of her earnings are exempt. (CCP § 703.580.) The San Francisco County Sheriff's Office is ordered to release the $396.17 in funds it is currently holding to Judgment Creditor towards satisfaction of the judgment. $160.00 is ordered to be withheld from Debtor's earnings per pay period until the debt is satisfied.
